title: In Front
description: Road North, ch.2. First real fight. The Fighter takes the front position without being asked.

steps.text: A pair of slimes had come down the road, iridescent in the wrong way, moving with more purpose than slimes had any right to. They were two paces out and closing.

fighter.text: The lead had already stepped forward. They had been moving toward the front since before anyone said the word *slime.* This was not new. This was the lead being the lead.

default.text: He didn't argue. He didn't explain. He didn't ask. He simply moved between the party and the road, sword up, shield angled, weight on the back foot like someone who had done this in a yard for years and now finally got to do it for real.
The slimes hit the shield. The shield held. The Fighter held. The party had a half-second longer to decide what to do, and a half-second was the entire fight.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: It's fine. I'm fine. Anyone hurt?

default.text: He had not looked at anyone while he asked, because looking would have meant taking his eyes off the road.

Later, when the others were checking the slime remains and arguing about iridescence, the Fighter looked at his own hands. They were shaking. He watched them for the space of a breath. Then he set them on the pommel of the sword and they were not shaking. He had not decided to do that. It was simply what the hands did when they were told.
The Fighter was, the party would learn, the kind of person who counted what mattered and trusted the rest.
